---
title: "Handouts"
author:

<!--  output: html_document -->
output:
  html_document:
    toc: true
    toc_depth: 3
    toc_float: 
      collapsed: false
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

This page lists and links to the required readings for the term. Readings might change as the term progresses. Refresh the page on your browser to see the latest version.

Dates indicate when the paper is to be <strong>discussed</strong>, so read beforehand.

<br>

## Discussion dates and papers

### January 13
[Wainer: How to display data badly](readings/wainer%201984%20amer%20stat%20-%20how%20to%20display%20data%20badly.pdf)

<br>

### January 20
[Hurlbert: Pseudoreplication and the design of experiments](readings/hurlbert 1984 ecol monogr - pseudoreplication.pdf)

<br>

### January 27
[Colegrave & Ruxton: Confidence intervals](readings/colegrave & ruxton 2003 behav ecol - use confidence intervals not post-hoc power when Ho not rejected.pdf)

Optional additional reading: [Hoenig & Heisey (2001)](readings/hoenig & heisey 2001 am stat - fallacy of power calculations for data analysis.pdf)

<br>

### February 3
[Kelly and Price: Correcting for regression to the mean](readings/kelly & price 2005 am nat - regression toward the mean.pdf)

Optional additional readings: [Forstmeier 2011](readings/forstmeier 2011 anat rec - brain size fallacy.pdf) and [Schluter 1992](https://www.zoology.ubc.ca/~schluter/reprints/schluter%201992%20nature%20-%20brain%20size%20differences.pdf) on a brain size fallacy.

<br>

### February 10
[Murtaugh: Simplicity in data analysis](readings/murtaugh 2007 ecology - simplicity and complexity in data analysis.pdf)

<br>

### February 24
[Verhoeven et al: Controlling false discovery rate](readings/verhoeven et al 2005 oikos - controlling the false discovery rate.pdf)

<br>

### March 3
[Whittingham et al: Why do we still use stepwise regression?](readings/whittingham et al 2006 jae - why not to use stepwise modeling.pdf)

Optional additional reading:&nbsp;[Mundry & Nunn: more on stepwise regression](readings/mundry et al 2009 am nat - more on why not to use stepwise model fitting.pdf)

<br>

### March 10
[Cohen: The earth is round (p < 0.05)](readings/cohen 1994 am psych - the earth is round.pdf)

<br>

### March 17
[Dochtermann & Jenkins: Working with multiple hypotheses](readings/dochtermann & jenkins 2011 bes - developing multiple hypotheses in behavioural ecology.pdf)

Optional additional reading:&nbsp;[Betini et al: why are we not testing multiple hypotheses](readings/bettini et al 2017 rsos - why are we not testing multiple hypotheses.pdf)

<br>

### March 24
[Palmer: Publication bias in fluctuating asymmetry](readings/palmer 1999 metaanalysis of fluctuating asymmetry and sexual selection.pdf)

<br>

### March 31
[Maynard et al: Multidimensional trait relationships](readings/maynard et al 2022 nature comm - global relationships in tree functional traits.pdf). 

<br>

### April 7
[Jetz et al: Phylogenetic distinctness and conservation](readings/jetz et al 2014 curr biol - conservation and phylogenetic distinctness.pdf)
